title: Sintro Sticheditor Program List
description: List of shooting programs ("Stiche") for the Sintro Trefferanzeige device. The on-device format is a binary .dat file with fixed 328-byte records; this schema describes the JSON representation used by the editor app, the CLI, and clipboard copy/paste. The list is a self-identifying, versioned envelope: { "format": "sintro-programs", "version": 1, "programs": [ ... ] }. Each program defines up to 8 sequential stages ("Passen"), where each stage specifies a target silhouette, scoring, fire method, posture, and optional series-fire timing. NOTE: one cross-field rule is NOT expressible in JSON Schema — once a step has shotNum=0, every following step must also have shotNum=0 (no gaps between active stages). The runtime validator in sticheditor.js enforces this.

format.description: Format discriminator. Always the literal "sintro-programs"; lets readers (e.g. clipboard paste) recognize the payload as a program list.

version.description: Format version. Currently 1. Bumped only on breaking changes to the envelope or program shape.

programs.description: The shooting programs, in list order (the app keeps them sorted by prgNum).

letter.description: A single ASCII letter, upper- or lowercase (A-Z or a-z). Stored on-device as one byte (the ASCII code).

capitalLetter.description: A single ASCII uppercase letter (A-Z only). Stored on-device as one byte.

vbaInteger.description: Signed 16-bit integer (the VBA Integer type used by the original Excel tool).

program.description: A single shooting program (Stich). Always contains exactly 8 step slots — unused trailing slots have shotNum=0 but must still carry valid placeholder values for the other fields (see step defaults).

prgNum.description: Program number (Stichnummer). Unique identifier shown to the shooter when selecting a program on the device. Must be 1-999.

title.description: Human-readable program title shown on the device and on printouts. In JSON this is the BARE title only — do NOT include the program number. On disk the .dat field is 32 chars and is always stored as `"NNN " + title` (zero-padded 3-digit prgNum + space + title); the parser strips that prefix on read and re-adds it on write. Max 28 ASCII chars in JSON (32 on disk minus the 4-char prefix).

internalId.description: Short internal identifier (max 8 ASCII chars). Stored in the Spare3 slot of the binary record and used as a stable program key independent of the user-visible title. May be empty.

printOffset.description: Vertical print offset applied to printed score sheets, in device-specific units. Typically 0; range is the full VBA Integer range.

printFormat.description: Print layout selector. Documented values: 'Z' = Links & Rechts (left & right), 'L' = Links, 'R' = Rechts, 'E' = Eingedrückt (compressed), 'H' = Hintereinander (sequential), 'T' = mit Schusskadenz (with shot cadence). Lowercase variants ('z','l','r','e','h','t') select the same layouts with a reduced header. 'S' = Spezial (special, undocumented), 'X' = Kalibrierprogramm (calibration program — not a real shooting program). Any other single letter validates but may not be recognized by the device.

weapon.description: Weapon type code (device-specific lookup; 0 if unspecified).

spare2.description: Reserved byte. Always 0 in all observed real-world records. Not exposed in the editor UI; set to 0 for new programs.

spareByte.description: Reserved byte of unknown purpose. Observed values: 48 (ASCII '0') and 49 (ASCII '1'); the device appears to expect it set. Default to 49 for newly created programs. Not exposed in the editor UI.

passeProgress.description: Per-stage session/progress data written by the Trefferanzeige device after the program has been used (originally named Spare1 in the VBA source). Exactly 8 strings of up to 7 chars each, one per step. For freshly authored programs, fill with eight empty or all-space strings; the device will overwrite this on first use. Preserve verbatim when round-tripping existing programs.

steps.description: Exactly 8 stages (Passen). Active stages come first; trailing inactive stages must have shotNum=0. Constraint not expressible here: once any step has shotNum=0, all later steps must also have shotNum=0 (no gaps). All 8 entries are validated even if inactive, so inactive slots still need valid letters/numbers in every field — copy a default step shape.

step.description: One stage (Passe) within a program. Active stages have shotNum>0; inactive trailing stages have shotNum=0 but still need valid values in every other field (validation runs on all 8 stages regardless).

shotNum.description: Number of shots in this stage. 0 marks the stage (and all subsequent stages) as inactive.

info.description: Short free-text label shown next to the stage (e.g. 'Probe', 'Match'). Max 8 ASCII chars; may be empty.

sil.description: Silhouette/target code — a single letter selecting which target image the device displays for this stage. The mapping from letter to target is device-specific.

calc.description: Scoring/calculation mode (Wertung). Signed 16-bit integer; MUST NOT be 0 (the original VBA rule is `Value <> 0 And IsNumeric`). The specific value selects a scoring scheme on the device. Default for new stages: 5.

fireMethod.description: Fire method (Feuerart) — a single uppercase letter (A-Z only; lowercase is rejected). Selects e.g. single fire vs. series fire on the device. Common default: 'E'.

printMode.description: Per-stage print mode — single letter (any case) controlling how this stage is rendered on the printout. Common default: 'N'.

stepTime1.description: Time limit for series fire in this stage, in device units (typically seconds). 0 means no time limit / not applicable. Two further time fields (StepTime2, StepTime3) exist in the binary format but are always 0 and are not represented here.

calcHighscore.description: Tiefschuss flag — whether low shots count toward the score. 0 = off, 1 = on.

position.description: Shooting position (German: Stellung) — ISSF terminology. UI/JSON values: 0 = prone (liegend), 1 = kneeling (kniend), 2 = standing (stehend). The on-disk binary uses different codes (1/3/4) and the VBA struct field is misleadingly named `FireMode`; the parser translates between the two — always use 0/1/2 here.

breakNotAllowed.description: Whether the device may pause/break BEFORE this stage starts. 1 = no break (default — proceed immediately from the previous stage), 0 = break allowed (free time before the first shot of this stage, used to encode an unlimited probe/warm-up phase). Common pattern: a program that allows free probe before active shooting has breakNotAllowed=0 on step 0 only; everything else stays 1. A leading P-step in human descriptions (e.g. "Probe frei + EF10×6") is NOT encoded as a separate step — it's expressed by setting breakNotAllowed=0 on the first active step.
